channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Senate Republicans vow action to boost border funds after briefing with Trump officials
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Citing warnings from President Donald Trump's border czar, Senate Republicans vowed Tuesday to move quickly on a budget plan that could pave the way for the passage of about $340 billion in additional border security and defense spending.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: In rare case, Georgia father jailed after his son was accused of a school shooting is granted bond
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — In a rare case of a parent being charged after a school shooting, a judge on Tuesday granted a $500,000 bond for the father of a 14-year-old boy accused of a deadly attack at a north Georgia high school.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Russia releases imprisoned American Marc Fogel in what US calls a step toward the end of Ukraine war
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Marc Fogel, an American teacher who was deemed wrongfully detained by Russia, has been released in what the White House described as a diplomatic thaw that could advance negotiations to end the war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump ally Steve Bannon pleads guilty and avoids jail time in border wall fraud case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Steve Bannon pleaded guilty on Tuesday to defrauding donors to a private effort to build a wall on the U.S. southern border, ending a case the conservative strategist decried as a “political persecution.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Crush of Valentine's Day flowers: Nearly a billion go through this city to you
item.description: If any husbands or boyfriends mess up Valentine's Day this week, it's not because of a shortage of flowers.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Trump signs executive order restoring plastic straw use in government
item.description: President Donald Trump signed an executive order directing the federal government to return to using plastic straws.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Karen Read can be retried in Boston officer John O’Keefe's death, court rules
item.description: The ruling from the Massachusetts Supreme Judicial Court clears the way for a new trial on all three charges.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: No, your student loans won't be forgiven if DOGE illegally accessed your data
item.description: No, you won't get your student loans forgiven if DOGE violated FERPA, even though TikTok videos claim otherwise.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, KRON4

item.title: Jet owned by Mötley Crüe singer in fatal collision at Arizona airport
item.description: One person was killed and others were injured when a private jet owned by Mötley Crüe singer Vince Neil collided with another jet Monday afternoon at the Scottsdale Airport in Arizona.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump seeks to expand his influence over arts and culture with Kennedy Center moves
item.description: After dominating U.S. politics for nearly a decade, President Trump now appears to be setting his sights on the country’s cultural landscape.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WGN 9

item.title: Salman Rushdie testifies about his shock and pain as an attacker repeatedly stabbed him on stage
item.description: Salman Rushdie has described in graphic detail the frenzied moments in 2022 when a masked man rushed at him on a stage in western New York and repeatedly slashed him with a knife, leaving him with life-threatening injuries.
item.dc:creator: Carolyn Thompson And Hillel Italie, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump's education chief comes from the boardroom, not the classroom. Backers say that's her strength
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Linda McMahon once described herself as an outsider stepping into the world of education, a business leader with an “inquiring mind” who would challenge the status quo and demand more for students.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump names Grenell as interim Kennedy Center executive director
item.description: President Trump on Monday named Richard Grenell, his envoy for special missions, the interim executive director of the Kennedy Center days after he named himself chair of a new board. “I am pleased to announce that Ric Grenell will serve as the Interim Executive Director of The Kennedy Center.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Election official in Arizona’s largest county signals a shift away from combating disinformation
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — One of the top election officials in Arizona's largest county, which has been roiled in recent years by conspiracy theories and intimidation of election workers, is moving away from the office's previous efforts to combat misinformation.
item.dc:creator: ALI SWENSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Your student loans won't be forgiven if DOGE violated FERPA
item.description: No, you won't get your student loans forgiven if DOGE violated FERPA, even though TikTok videos are claiming otherwise.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Combat your post-Super Bowl fatigue with these freebies, discounts
item.description: Even though the Super Bowl is over, several restaurants and chains are offering freebies to help football fans recover from Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KRON4

item.title: Space telescope spots rare 'Einstein ring' of light around galaxy in our cosmic neighborhood
item.description: Europe’s Euclid space telescope has detected a rare halo of bright light around a nearby galaxy, astronomers reported Monday.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN AP Aerospace Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Member of Kendrick Lamar's Super Bowl halftime show won't face charges for flag protest
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — The cast member who displayed the Palestinian flag with the word “GAZA” in protest during Kendrick Lamar's Super Bowl halftime show will not face charges, New Orleans police said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump sued after quietly firing top whistleblower protector
item.description: President Trump late Friday fired the leader of an office designed to protect government whistleblowers, a detail revealed in a Monday lawsuit from the head of the Office of the Special Counsel (OSC).
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WGN 9

item.title: Member of Kendrick Lamar's Super Bowl halftime show won't face charges for flag protest
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — The cast member who displayed the Palestinian flag with the word “GAZA” in protest during Kendrick Lamar's Super Bowl halftime show will not face charges, New Orleans police said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Super Bowl LIX highlights: Memorable plays, moving tributes and a historic presidential visit
item.description: The Philadelphia Eagles defeated the Kansas City Chiefs 40-22 in the first Super Bowl to be held in New Orleans in a dozen years. It was played amid heavy security — and with President Donald Trump in attendance — just 41 days after a man plowed a truck into New Year's revelers on Bourbon Street.
item.dc:creator: Brett Martel, KRON4

item.title: President Trump’s next target: the penny
item.description: President Trump claims he has instructed the U.S. Treasury Department to stop producing pennies, calling the 1 cent coin wasteful. Trump announced his actions in a Truth Social post Sunday night. “For far too long the United States has minted pennies which literally cost us more than 2 cents.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KTLA 5

item.title: Senate Democrats launch portal for whistleblowers in Trump administration
item.description: Senate Democratic Leader Chuck Schumer (D-N.Y.) and Sen. Gary Peters (D-Mich.), the ranking member of the Senate Homeland Security Committee, said they are launching a new portal for whistleblowers to expose wrongdoing and abuses of power in the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Taylor Swift booed by heavily pro-Eagles crowd at the Super Bowl
item.description: Taylor Swift was back at the Super Bowl to watch Travis Kelce and his Kansas City Chiefs play the Philadelphia Eagles on Sunday. Her reception was a whole lot different than last year.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump official orders consumer protection agency to stop work
item.description: The Trump administration has ordered the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau to stop nearly all its work, effectively shutting down an agency that was created to protect consumers after the 2008 financial crisis and subprime mortgage-lending scandal.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: WATCH: Snow blankets parts of Northeast US, up to a foot expected for some
item.description: A strong storm is expected to bring moderate to heavy snowfall across parts of the Upper Great Lakes on Saturday before intensifying overnight across the Northeast.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Musk calls for impeachment of judge who blocked DOGE access at Treasury
item.description: Elon Musk is calling for the impeachment of the federal judge who made a decision early Saturday morning that the Treasury Department should block access to anyone “other than civil servants with a need for access to perform their job duties” from its payment systems.
item.dc:creator: Ian Swanson, KRON4

item.title: House Republicans mull taxes on scholarships and changes to student loan programs
item.description: As Republicans in Congress look for ways to slash spending, some legislators are floating new taxes on college scholarships, an end to student loan repayment plans and a big hike in taxes on university endowments.
item.dc:creator: CHEYANNE MUMPHREY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Nelly delivers hits at 'Homecoming' Super Bowl week concert in historic New Orleans restaurant
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Nelly brought his signature St. Louis swagger to a New Orleans institution, turning a nearly seven-decade-old restaurant into a Super Bowl week party. The multi-Grammy winner packed Brennan’s on Friday night, delivering a hit-filled set at the h.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN LANDRUM Jr., Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Musk says he has no plans to buy TikTok
item.description: Despite ByteDance stating that they are not planning to sell, some investors have shown interest in buying the company.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, KRON4

item.title: He's back, baby! ESPN's Dick Vitale makes return to commentating following 4th bout with cancer
item.description: ESPN’s Dick Vitale received a standing ovation Saturday night at Clemson’s Littlejohn Coliseum in his first game back as a commentator since recovering from his fourth bout with cancer.
item.dc:creator: STEVE REED, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Anthony Davis leaves his Dallas debut with an injury after seismic Luka Doncic trade
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — Anthony Davis left in the third quarter with a lower-body injury and didn't return in his debut with the Dallas Mavericks on Saturday after the seismic trade that sent Luka Doncic to the Los Angeles Lakers.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Philadelphia radio station bans Taylor Swift music ahead of Super Bowl
item.description: A release from the station claims that 94.5 is "pressing pause on all things Taylor Swift." The announcement came after a Swift fan and a Philly advertising agency spent the week trolling each other with ads in KC and Philly.
item.dc:creator: Dillon Seckington, KRON4

item.title: Super Bowl LIX: Taylor Swift, Travis Kelce spotted out in New Orleans
item.description: Power couple Taylor Swift and Kansas City Chiefs Tight End Travis Kelce were spotted on Friday night eating at a restaurant while in New Orleans for Super Bowl LIX.
item.dc:creator: Kenny Lopez, KRON4

item.title: PlayStation Network back up after dealing with technical issues since Friday
item.description: (KRON) -- The PlayStation Network announced late Saturday afternoon that "all services are up and running" after it experienced issues across its platforms for almost 24 hours. Users were first alerted of service issues at 4 p.m. on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Tor Smith, KRON4

item.title: Missing Alaska flight found with no survivors: What to know
item.description: The plane, operated by Bering Air, had been traveling from Unalakleet to Nome when it vanished on Thursday, only to be found the next day.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, KRON4

item.title: Super Bowl prices are down this year. StubHub explains why
item.description: If you’re still planning to attend the Super Bowl on Sunday, you can expect a price drop compared to last year.
item.dc:creator: JOHN CARUCCI, Associated Press, Fox 5
